title: BoundaryFillFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing boundary fill features in a component and supports the ability to create new boundary fill features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new boundary fill feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a BoundaryFillFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the boundary fill you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the BoundaryFillFeatureInput object.   To determine the possible boundaries and allow you to choose which cells to keep, the boundary fill feature does a partial compute when the input object is created. To do this it starts a boundary fill feature transaction and completes the transaction when you call the add method. If you don't call the add method to finish the transaction it leaves Fusion in a bad state and there will be undo problems and possibly a crash. If you have created a BoundFillFeatureInput object and don't want to finish the feature creation, you need to call the cancel method on the BoundaryFillFeatureInput object to safely abort the current boundary fill transaction.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified boundary fill feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified boundary fill feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of boundary fill features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Boundary Fill Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boundary fill feature.

add.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new boundary fill feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created BoundaryFillFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A BoundaryFillFeatureInput object that defines the desired boundary fill feature. Use the createInput method to create a new BoundaryFillFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the BoundaryFillFeatureInput object) to define the boundary fill feature.

related_samples.name: boundaryFillFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the boundaryFill.add method. To use this sample you need to have two existing overlapping bodies. You'll be prompted to select the bodies when running the script.

related_samples.name: Boundary Fill Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boundary fill feature.

classType.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a BoundaryFillFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the boundary fill you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the BoundaryFillFeatureInput object. To determine the possible boundaries and allow you to choose which cells to keep, the boundary fill feature does a partial compute when the input object is created. To do this it starts a boundary fill feature transaction and completes the transaction when you call the add method. If you don't call the add method to finish the transaction it leaves Fusion in a bad state and there will be undo problems and possibly a crash. If you have created a BoundFillFeatureInput object and don't want to finish the feature creation, you need to call the cancel method on the BoundaryFillFeatureInput object to safely abort the current boundary fill transaction.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created BoundaryFillFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: tools
parameters.description: A collection of one or more construction planes and open or closed BRepBody objects that will be used in calculating the possible closed boundaries.

related_samples.name: boundaryFillFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the boundaryFill.add method. To use this sample you need to have two existing overlapping bodies. You'll be prompted to select the bodies when running the script.

related_samples.name: Boundary Fill Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boundary fill feature.

item.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified boundary fill feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified boundary fill feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of boundary fill features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = boundaryFillFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: BoundaryFillFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = boundaryFillFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
